test_cases.description: Noir warehouse investigator — true crime podcast cover, cold monochrome with amber accent, square format
test_cases.input_summary: Classic film noir aesthetic, specific lighting setup (amber flashlight in darkness), emotional register (tension/isolation), square podcast format
test_cases.output_summary: Primary prompt with precise shadow-to-light ratio (75% shadow); lighting diagram in words; aesthetic notes table explaining each noir element; negative prompts exclude horror-kitsch and digital sharpness; alternative offers gothic outdoor variant

test_cases.description: Gothic cemetery at night — horror anthology podcast, moonlight, architectural dark
test_cases.input_summary: Gothic aesthetic (distinct from noir), cemetery environment, moonlight as key light source, cold silver-blue palette
test_cases.output_summary: Prompt correctly distinguishes gothic from noir (architectural scale, moonlight not streetlamp, romantic darkness not crime darkness); specific environmental details (iron gates, crumbling stone, yew trees); negative prompts exclude cheap horror clichés

test_cases.description: Industrial urban decay — investigative journalism newsletter header, grimdark without horror
test_cases.input_summary: Industrial aesthetic, grimdark register, urban decay as subject, newsletter 3:1 header format, melancholy not horror
test_cases.output_summary: Prompt uses abandoned factory environment with specific industrial details (broken skylights, oxidized steel, standing water reflecting sky); emotional register correctly set as melancholy not horror; negative prompt excludes both jump-scare horror and dystopian sci-fi aesthetics

notes: The skill correctly distinguishes between different flavors of dark aesthetic — the most important quality differentiator. Lighting precision is the strongest element: specifying shadow-to-light ratios and exact light source positions produces better results than general 'dark' instructions. Thumbnail legibility note for podcast artwork is a useful practical addition.
